# AI Quant Letter #3 — All Negative Momentum Scores, ETH Still Leads

*Weekly UPbit Crypto Signals — auto-generated by [maiupbit](https://pypi.org/project/maiupbit/) (open source, Apache 2.0)*

---

## The Signal This Week: Hold Cash

Every coin in the dual momentum top 5 has a negative score. That is the clearest "stay out" signal the model can give. No rotation target exists — the best-ranked coin (ETH at -0.057) is still underwater.

This is the third consecutive week where the dual momentum model has not produced a buy signal.

## Market Season Context

| Metric | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Season | 🟢 Bullish (Month 3) |
| Halving Cycle | Mid-cycle (Day 695 post-halving) |
| Season Multiplier | 1.2× |

The macro season indicator still reads bullish. But "bullish season" does not mean "buy everything now." The season multiplier adjusts position sizing — it does not override momentum signals. When short-term momentum is negative across the board, the system respects that regardless of the macro backdrop.

This tension between macro-bullish and micro-bearish is exactly where systematic rules earn their keep. Discretionary traders often override caution during bullish seasons. The model does not.

## Dual Momentum Rankings

| Rank | Coin | Score |
|------|------|-------|
| 🥇 | **ETH** | -0.057 |
| 🥈 | **BTC** | -0.061 |
| 🥉 | **LINK** | -0.098 |
| 4 | **AVAX** | -0.105 |
| 5 | **SOL** | -0.112 |

**Reading the scores:** Dual momentum combines absolute momentum (is this asset trending up?) with relative momentum (which asset is trending best?). All five are negative, meaning none of them pass the absolute momentum threshold. The ranking only tells you which is *least bad* — not which is good.

ETH and BTC are clustered near zero (-0.057 vs -0.061). If either crosses back above zero next week, that would be the first rotation signal in three weeks.

## Multi-Factor Rankings

| Rank | Coin | Score |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | **ETH** | 0.554 |
| 2 | **AVAX** | 0.527 |
| 3 | **BTC** | 0.467 |
| 4 | **LINK** | 0.443 |
| 5 | **SOL** | 0.255 |

The multi-factor model (which blends trend, volatility, volume, and on-chain metrics) is more optimistic. ETH and AVAX score above 0.5, suggesting the underlying structure is not as weak as pure momentum implies.

The divergence between momentum (all negative) and multi-factor (mostly positive) often appears during consolidation phases — the market has stopped falling but has not started a new leg up. These periods can resolve in either direction.

## What Changed From Last Week

- **ETH** held the #1 spot in both models. Consistency matters — when the same asset leads across different signal types, it is the strongest candidate for the next rotation.
- **SOL** remains the weakest in both models. The gap between SOL's multi-factor score (0.255) and the leader (0.554) is the widest in the top 5.
- **AVAX** jumped to multi-factor #2, suggesting improving fundamentals even while momentum lags.

## The System's Edge

Three weeks of "hold cash" is boring. That is the point.

The maiupbit quant engine runs every week whether or not there is an exciting narrative. It does not chase news. It does not get anxious during consolidation. It waits for the math to confirm before allocating.

If you are building your own quant signals, the entire engine is open source:

- **PyPI:** `pip install maiupbit`
- **GitHub:** [github.com/jini92/M.AI.UPbit](https://github.com/jini92/M.AI.UPbit)
- **Stack:** Python + LSTM + GPT-4o analysis + UPbit API
